query: Turkish cilbir poached eggs with garlicky yogurt and Aleppo pepper butter

draft.title: Turkish Eggs (Cilbir)
draft.description: Poached eggs served over garlicky yogurt, topped with melted butter infused with Aleppo pepper for a rich, tangy Turkish breakfast.

steps.text: In a bowl, mix the plain yogurt with minced garlic and a pinch of salt. Set aside to let the flavors meld.
Bring a large pot of water to a gentle simmer. Add white vinegar to the water to help eggs coagulate.
Crack eggs one at a time into a small bowl, then gently slide each into the simmering water. Poach for 3-4 minutes until whites are set but yolks remain runny.
While eggs poach, melt the butter in a small pan over low heat. Stir in Aleppo pepper and remove from heat.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully remove eggs from water and drain on paper towels.
Spread the garlicky yogurt evenly on a serving plate or individual plates.
Place poached eggs on top of the yogurt. Drizzle the Aleppo pepper butter over the eggs.
Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Garnish with chopped dill and parsley.
Serve immediately with rustic bread slices for dipping.

warnings: Use fresh eggs for best poaching results.
Adjust Aleppo pepper amount to control spiciness.
